study of animals and of their impact on human behaviour. Even
trees and creepers are supposed to presage coming events. It is to be
remembered that there are hundreds of references to auguries and
portents in the Rdmdyana and the Mahdbharata. It is claimed that
even in the Vedas there are such references. Pdnini refers to them
in many places.1 The ancients observed minutely the nest-building
activity of crows in particular months in order to predict future
prospects for the country. Prediction of rain and pests is made on
the basis of these nests.
The author gives in this connection a useful list of synonyms of
birds and beasts.2
During our author's time, it is presumed, women were prac-
tising astrology and sooth-saying. They were called Ikfanikds.
We can make a fair surmise about the commencement of sea-
sons during the author's time. According to him the year began
with Sisira (cold season).3 But Garga opines that it begins with
Vasanta, the spring.
